title: adenostyles albifrons subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Adenostyles albifrons subsp. pyrenaica, also known as the Alpine hawk's beard, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its tall, perennial stems and clusters of white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Adenostyles albifrons subsp. pyrenaica include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to aid in wound healing.
